summary: This brief dictionary-style entry introduces the term 'detestable' as a portmanteau meaning software that cannot be tested. Fowler attributes the coinage to an unnamed member of the Sydney XP group. The post uses wordplay to highlight that untestable software is, by implication, worthy of contempt. It's a characteristically concise Fowler 'bliki' entry that packages a significant software quality principle into a memorable term.
key_points: Untestable software deserves its own pejorative term
The pun equates 'not testable' with 'deserving hatred'
Good software design inherently supports testability
key_insight: Software that cannot be tested is software that deserves contempt—testability is not optional, it's a fundamental quality attribute.
